author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
prabhura vacana, śuniyā takhana,
śrīvāsa loṭāñā bhūmi
bale, śuna nātha! tava rasa-bhaṅga,
sahite nā pāri āmi

ekaṭī tanaya, mariyāche nātha,
tāhe mora kibā duḥkha
jadi saba mare, tomāre heriyā,
tabu ta’ pāiba sukha

tava nṛtya-bhaṅga, hoile āmāra,
maraṇa hoita, hari!
tāi kusaṁvāda, nā dila tomāre,
vipad āśaṅkā kari’

ebe ājñā deha, mṛta suta la’ye,
satkāra karuna sabe
eteka śuniyā, gorā dvijamaṇi,
kā̐dite lāgila tabe

kemane e sabe, chāḍiyā jāiba,
parāṇa vikala haya
se⋅kathā śuniyā, bhakativinoda,
manete pāila bhaya
en_translation:
Hearing the Lord's words, Śrīvāsa then prostrated himself on the ground and said, "Listen, my Lord! I cannot bear to see Your spiritual absorption broken.

One son, my Lord, has died; what sorrow is that to me? Even if everyone were to die, if I can only behold You, I will still find happiness.

If Your dancing were interrupted, O Lord, that would be my true death! Therefore, fearing misfortune, no bad news was given to You.

Now give Your permission; taking the dead son, let everyone perform the funeral rites." Hearing this, Lord Caitanya, the jewel of the twice-born, then began to cry.

"How can I leave such devotees?" His heart became distressed. Hearing those words, Bhaktivinoda felt fear in his heart.
